pages.full_text:
bored in it as a candle holder. The old miners wore leather aprons at the back — arse leathers — to sit on while they worked. In 1881 they had a German lift where men could go up or down by stepping sideways onto the next rung — Until 16[?00], 20[?–] kg rock could be [?lifted] — 20 m. by a teenager using a bucket & winding handle. He was paid less than the trained breakers at the bottom. In 1680 they started using horse winches. 2 or 3 horses went round, hired from [illegible] local farms. Each farm supplied horses to work 1 week in a month. In 1880 they started using water power. The same water was used to work 18 machines on its way down the hill.
        Safety binding on skis started in 1950s
We saw the ski long jump records
9 m. in 1810 to 209 in 1994.
        Simon & Eve went to the Baroque church, but Simon got huffed at having to pay so we walked out. Lovely waterfall in the town, river near bridge. John found drill noises in good mock-up of mine very loud. William noted a Czech or Hung. crystal decanter, used to pay ski-book royalties to a Norwegian after the war, now worth £100,000.
pages.notes: This page is a continuation of text from the previous spread — the top of the page begins mid-sentence ('bored in it as a candle holder'). Content describes a mine museum visit with historical information about mining practices. Mentions a Baroque church, a waterfall, and a river near a bridge — location not named on this page but context suggests a Central/Eastern European town (Czech or Hungarian crystal decanter mentioned). The sketch of a horse winch (triangle with arrow) appears mid-text. '16[?00]' may read '1600'; '20[?–] kg' unclear abbreviation. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner.

pages.full_text: Charles picture of Transport  Aug 12th Tuesday
headings: Charles picture of Transport  Aug 12th Tuesday
pages.notes: Right page contains a heading in blue/black ink at the top: 'Charles picture of Transport Aug 12th Tuesday', followed by a child's pencil sketch of a wheeled transport vehicle (appears to be a crane or construction vehicle/truck). A few isolated blue ink letters/numbers appear at bottom of page (u, dashes, o) — possibly incomplete notation. Aug 12th fell on a Tuesday in 1997 and 2003 within the volume's date range (1989–2003); 1997 is preferred given the left-page mention of '1994' records as recent, but 2003 cannot be excluded. No handwritten page number visible in top-right corner.
